div.head: The Ambassador in El Salvador (Thurston) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: Supplementing despatch no. 1561, dated May 13, 1944,Not printed. I have the honor to report that Mr. RenwickWilliam W. Renwick, representative of the Fiscal Agent. advised me today that the Fiscal Agent under the Salvador loan contract has filed a protest with the Salvadoran Government and with the Department of State Supra. against the proposed debt settlement under negotiation between agents of the Salvadoran Government and the Foreign Bondholders Protective Council Inc.

Mr. Renwick added that he has submitted a memorandum to the Salvadoran authorities on the subject (he stated he would give me a copy of the memorandum),Copy transmitted to the Department by the Ambassador in his despatch 1599, May 20, 1944; neither printed. suggesting that the proposed settlement be abandoned, describing the settlement as an ex-parte arrangement in which the bondholders had no voice, and urging that as a provisional measure service be resumed on the bonds on the basis of the Readjustment Agreement of April 27, 1936.
